metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

coverage.verify_error: step 'review@then/verify_branch@then/verify' failed: the LLM provider call failed: Streamed response ended without content or tool calls

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit test scenario (e.g. `test_error_message_content`) to the test coverage list that asserts the error output contains 'file_impact' and a remediation hint about the `rebar-ticket:` trailer.
findings.finding: AC5 ('error message names the reason and remediation') has no mapped test scenario in the Scope section — 'coverage for block/pass/skip/force-close' does not include message-content verification, leaving this criterion unreachable by the described steps.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Scope bullet: '…coverage for block/pass/skip/force-close, and a message-content assertion verifying the reason (file_impact) and remediation (rebar-ticket: trailer) appear in stderr.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a proving command (e.g. `pytest tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_completion_gate.py -v`) to the Scope or a 'How to verify' section so done-ness is mechanically demonstrable.
findings.finding: No concrete proving command is given anywhere in the plan — the Scope says 'coverage for block/pass/skip/force-close' but never specifies a command (e.g. `pytest tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_completion_gate.py`) that an executor or reviewer could run to produce evidence of success.
findings.suggested_fix: Append to the Scope: 'Run `pytest tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_completion_gate.py -v` — all five scenarios must pass with no LLM call being made in the block case (assert via mock/spy).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Expand AC2 to cover both trailer formats (`rebar-ticket:` and `<id>:`) or add a separate criterion for the `<id>:` path, matching the scope's dual-resolver description.
findings.finding: AC2 specifies 'rebar-ticket trailer' only, but the Scope describes the resolver handling both `rebar-ticket:` AND `<id>:` trailer formats. The acceptance criterion is narrower than the implementation, leaving the `<id>:` trailer path without a covering criterion.
findings.location: Scope section (resolver description) vs AC line 2
findings.suggested_fix: AC2: 'The same ticket closes once a commit references it via a `rebar-ticket:` or `<id>:` trailer (both resolver paths verified).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define explicit error handling in `_referencing_commit_exists()`: catch subprocess errors (non-zero exit, timeout, FileNotFoundError), log a warning, and specify the fallback behavior (fail-open or fail-closed) so the close gate degrades predictably rather than crashing.
findings.finding: The `_referencing_commit_exists()` helper runs `git log` as a subprocess, but the plan specifies no error handling for subprocess failures (not in a git repo, permission error, timeout on large history, binary not found). An unhandled failure will either crash the close gate or spuriously block every close attempt, silently breaking an unrelated workflow.
findings.suggested_fix: Wrap the git subprocess call in a try/except (CalledProcessError, FileNotFoundError, TimeoutExpired); on failure, log a warning at WARNING level and return a sentinel (e.g., raise a distinct PreCheckError) so `_completion_precheck` can surface a clear message rather than propagating a raw exception.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one structured log statement in `_completion_precheck` (and in `_referencing_commit_exists()`) recording the precheck outcome (blocked/passed/skipped) at DEBUG or INFO level, so the decision is visible in log aggregation without relying solely on the CLI exit message.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new failure point (git subprocess in the close gate) but specifies no observability — no log line, metric, or trace for when the precheck blocks, passes, or errors. Operators and authors cannot tell from logs why a close was blocked or whether the precheck fired at all.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief design-doc or CLAUDE.md entry (or inline ADR comment in `_completion_precheck`) recording the decision: 'file_impact tickets require a referencing commit before completion verification; this mirrors the open-children guard pattern,' so future maintainers can apply the pattern consistently.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new close-gate contract (commit-reference required for file_impact tickets) but mentions no update to CLAUDE.md, an ADR, or any design document. This is a novel business rule tying VCS history to ticket lifecycle — future maintainers have no record of why the rule exists or how it is expected to evolve (e.g., whether other impact types will gain similar guards).
findings.location: Plan overall — no doc-update step mentioned

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the error-handling policy for `_referencing_commit_exists()` when `git log` fails (subprocess error, non-repo, shallow history): define whether the precheck fails open, fails closed with a clear message, or propagates — and add a corresponding AC or test case.
findings.finding: The plan does not specify how `_referencing_commit_exists()` behaves when the `git log` invocation itself fails (e.g., not in a git repo, git binary absent, shallow clone with insufficient history). A subprocess error could propagate as an unhandled exception, silently fail open and allow the close to proceed, or silently fail closed and block unnecessarily — none of which is specified.
findings.suggested_fix: Add an explicit policy: on git subprocess failure, fail closed with a descriptive error naming the git failure as the cause, and add an AC: 'If git log cannot be executed, the precheck fails with an actionable error message rather than an unhandled exception.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm that `_referencing_commit_exists()` reuses `extract_ticket_refs()` from `rebar._commands.verify_commit` (plus `resolve_ticket_id`) rather than re-implementing the trailer-parsing and id-resolution loop, so the two gates cannot drift.
findings.finding: The proposed `_referencing_commit_exists()` would re-implement commit-message parsing logic (scan `git log`, extract `rebar-ticket:` trailers and `<id>:` subject tokens, resolve each via the shared resolver) that is already provided by `extract_ticket_refs()` + `verify_commit_message()` in `src/rebar/_commands/verify_commit.py` (lines 55–109).
findings.suggested_fix: Import `extract_ticket_refs` from `rebar._commands.verify_commit` and call it on each commit message from `git log --format=%B`; resolve each extracted candidate via `resolve_ticket_id`. This keeps the single source of truth for trailer parsing in `verify_commit.py` and avoids duplication.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify whether _referencing_commit_exists() reuses extract_ticket_refs() from verify_commit.py for commit-message parsing, or documents why a separate implementation is needed.
findings.finding: The plan asserts _referencing_commit_exists() will resolve commits' rebar-ticket:/<id>: refs 'through the shared resolver', but the canonical commit-message extraction function (extract_ticket_refs in src/rebar/_commands/verify_commit.py) is not referenced by name. The plan's inline description ('rebar-ticket:/<id>: refs') matches what extract_ticket_refs implements (including safety guards and de-duplication), but the plan neither names it nor says it will be reused — leaving it unspecified whether the helper reuses the existing function or re-implements equivalent logic (risking drift from the canonical parser).
findings.suggested_fix: Import and call extract_ticket_refs from rebar._commands.verify_commit in _referencing_commit_exists() to parse each commit's message, rather than re-implementing the rebar-ticket:/<id>: extraction inline.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the git log invocation scope for _referencing_commit_exists() (e.g. --all vs current branch, depth/since limit, cwd/repo_root threading) to make the helper's behavior deterministic across branch and clone configurations.
findings.finding: The plan asserts _referencing_commit_exists() will 'scan git log' but gives no specification of the git log invocation scope (which branch, how far back, whether --all or only the current branch is scanned). No existing codebase precedent for a bulk git log scan in the _commands layer was found (no matches for 'git log' in src/rebar). Without a specified scope, the helper's behavior is undefined for common real-world cases: a ticket whose referencing commit is on a different branch, a shallow clone, or a repo with a very long history.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name `src/rebar/_commands/verify_commit.py` explicitly in the Scope as a reuse dependency (preferred: call `extract_ticket_refs`/`verify_commit_message`) or justify an independent re-implementation — either way, the plan must state which approach is taken.
findings.finding: The plan says `_referencing_commit_exists()` will 'resolve each commit's `rebar-ticket:`/`<id>:` refs through the shared resolver', but does not name `src/rebar/_commands/verify_commit.py` (which already owns exactly this logic via `extract_ticket_refs` and `verify_commit_message`) as either a reuse dependency or an edit target. If the new helper reimplements trailer parsing independently, two diverging implementations of the `rebar-ticket:` trailer grammar will exist; if it calls into `verify_commit.py`, that file is an unenumerated consumer/dependency.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Scope section: '`_referencing_commit_exists()` will reuse `verify_commit.extract_ticket_refs` and `resolve_ticket_id` rather than reimplementing the trailer grammar; no edits to `verify_commit.py` itself are needed.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in the plan which git repository `_referencing_commit_exists()` runs `git log` against (the source-code repo at `repo_root`, using `-C repo_root`), and whether it searches all refs (e.g. `--all`) or only merged-to-HEAD ancestry — and justify the chosen scope as correct for the single-dev flow described in the existing close-gate rationale.
findings.finding: The plan says `_referencing_commit_exists()` 'scans `git log`' but does not specify (a) which git repository to run the log against (the source-code repo at `repo_root` vs. the tracker repo), or (b) whether to search all branches/refs (`--all`) vs. only the current branch's ancestry. Without `--all`, a commit on a feature branch not yet merged to the active branch would be invisible, causing a false block even when the implementation has landed on a non-default branch.
findings.suggested_fix: Run `git -C <repo_root> log --all --format=%B` (or `git log --all --grep='rebar-ticket:'` for a pre-filtered pass) so commits on feature branches are visible. Document that `--all` is used so a commit on any local branch satisfies the check, consistent with the 'state about to be pushed' framing.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a positive rationale in the Scope or Why section explaining why `git log` scanning is chosen over `state['commits']` (e.g., COMMITS events are opt-in and not guaranteed at close time, so only a live `git log` scan provides a reliable ground truth).
findings.finding: The plan provides no positive rationale for choosing `git log` scanning over the existing `state['commits']` field (populated via `attach_commits` / COMMITS events already tracked in the reducer). Without a stated rationale, an implementer cannot confidently choose between these two data sources if they encounter both.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm that `_referencing_commit_exists()` reuses `verify_commit.extract_ticket_refs` (and its `_SAFE_TOKEN` path-safety guard) for per-commit message parsing rather than reimplementing trailer extraction inline.
findings.finding: The plan's `_referencing_commit_exists()` helper is described as scanning `git log` and resolving `rebar-ticket:/<id>:` refs 'through the shared resolver', but does not explicitly call out reuse of `verify_commit.extract_ticket_refs` — the canonical, already-present function that owns the trailer-vs-subject-prefix parsing rules AND the `_SAFE_TOKEN` path-safety guard. A parallel reimplementation would duplicate logic and drop the security guard.
findings.suggested_fix: In the Scope section, replace 'resolves each commit's rebar-ticket:/<id>: refs through the shared resolver' with 'calls `verify_commit.extract_ticket_refs(message)` for each commit message (reusing the canonical trailer + subject-prefix parser and its _SAFE_TOKEN guard), then resolves each candidate via `resolve_ticket_id`'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the `git log` format flags (e.g. `--format=%B` or `--format=%H%n%B`) needed to extract full commit message bodies for `extract_ticket_refs`, and state whether `--all` / branch-scope is required to cover commits not on the current branch.
findings.finding: The plan says `_referencing_commit_exists()` 'scans git log' but does not specify the `--format` flag required to extract full commit message bodies for `extract_ticket_refs`. The default `git log` output is a decorated summary (not message bodies), so `rebar-ticket:` trailers would not appear in the output fed to the parser — the integration would silently fail to find any references.
findings.location: Scope section — `_referencing_commit_exists()` description: 'scans `git log`, resolves each commit's `rebar-ticket:`/`<id>:` refs through the shared resolver'
findings.suggested_fix: Use `git log --format=%B` (full body per commit, blank-line-separated) or `git log --format=%H%x00%B` (NUL-delimited) to emit complete message text. The existing pattern `git show -s --format=%B <rev>` in verify_commit.py:140 is the proven per-commit analogue; a `git log --format=%B --all` scan would be the multi-commit equivalent. Document the chosen scope (current branch vs `--all`) in the helper docstring.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that `_referencing_commit_exists` reuses `extract_ticket_refs` from `rebar._commands.verify_commit` (rather than re-implementing trailer parsing) so the canonical `_TRAILER` regex, `_SAFE_TOKEN` guard, and subject-token fallback cannot drift.
findings.finding: The plan references 'resolves each commit's rebar-ticket:/<id>: refs through the shared resolver' but does not specify whether `_referencing_commit_exists` will import and reuse the existing `extract_ticket_refs` function from `verify_commit.py` or independently re-implement trailer parsing. These are co-located in different modules; without explicit co-location direction, an implementor may duplicate the parsing logic and drift from the canonical `_TRAILER` regex / `_SAFE_TOKEN` guard defined in `verify_commit.py`.
findings.location: Scope section — `_referencing_commit_exists()` description: 'resolves each commit's rebar-ticket:/<id>: refs through the shared resolver'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Scope: '`_referencing_commit_exists` extracts trailer refs via `extract_ticket_refs` (imported from `rebar._commands.verify_commit`) and resolves each via `resolve_ticket_id` — no new trailer-parsing logic.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a fallback specification for `git log` subprocess failures in `_referencing_commit_exists`: state whether a git error should fail-closed (block with a descriptive CommandError) or fail-open (skip the commit check with a warning), consistent with the rest of `_completion_precheck`'s fail-closed posture.
findings.finding: The plan specifies no fallback posture when the `git log` subprocess inside `_referencing_commit_exists` fails (e.g., git not available, not inside a git repo, timeout, corrupt object DB). Because this precheck blocks ticket closure on exit 1, the fail-open vs fail-closed decision on git errors is load-bearing but absent from the plan.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Scope: 'If the `git log` call fails (subprocess error, timeout, non-git directory), `_referencing_commit_exists` raises `CommandError` with exit 1 naming the infra failure — consistent with the fail-closed posture of the surrounding gate.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
